advantages: Trunk-based / GitHub Flow are simple, fast, and align well with continuous deployment.
GitFlow handles parallel versioned releases (libraries, mobile apps) cleanly.
Environment branches (GitLab Flow) give staged rollout without full GitFlow complexity.
A documented workflow removes recurring 'how do we land this?' arguments.
disadvantages: Over-process: GitFlow at a 5-person startup adds friction without payoff.
Trunk-based without feature flags is fragile — one broken commit blocks everyone.
Long-lived branches (GitFlow's `release/*`) accumulate conflict and integration debt.
Switching workflows mid-project is painful; pick deliberately upfront.
